steps.title: First account deposits
steps.content: Depositing $x$ dollars once per month for 12 months contributes $12x$ dollars annually.

steps.title: Second account deposits
steps.content: Depositing $y$ dollars twice per month for 12 months means $2 \times 12 = 24$ deposits, contributing $24y$ dollars annually.

steps.title: Set up the total equation
steps.content:
The total across both accounts is $4,300:
$$4300 = 12x + 24y$$

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$4,300 = 12x + 12y$': This would mean $y$ is deposited only once per month, not twice. ✗
  • Choice B — '$4,300 = 12x + 24y$': $12x$ for once-monthly $x$ deposits, $24y$ for twice-monthly $y$ deposits. ✓
  • Choice C — '$4,300 = 24x + 12y$': This reverses the deposit frequencies — $x$ would be twice-monthly and $y$ once-monthly. ✗
  • Choice D — '$4,300 = 24x + 24y$': This would mean both accounts receive deposits twice per month. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
